summary: This is a very brief news post with two items: an announcement about Joel speaking at the Irish Java Technology Conference in Dublin, and a link to Stefan's article series about Wasabi, Fog Creek's in-house programming language. The post is mostly notable for the humorous aside about how Wasabi got its name, having been renamed from 'FogBasic' after an internal naming contest. The contest came down to 'Bone Crusher 3000' vs 'Wasabi,' with Wasabi winning despite the appeal of typing 'bc3k' into the command line.
key_points: Joel announced a speaking engagement at the Irish Java Technology Conference in November 2007
Fog Creek's in-house language Wasabi was originally called 'FogBasic' before being renamed via an internal contest
The naming contest used instant run-off voting, with the final two candidates being 'Bone Crusher 3000' and 'Wasabi'
money_quotes: During the summer of 2006, we realized that I was a complete dork for naming it FogBasic, so we had a naming contest.
The final two were Bone Crusher 3000 and Wasabi. As much as I wanted to be typing bc3k into the command line, Wasabi won out.
key_insight: A lighthearted news update that gives a glimpse into Fog Creek's playful engineering culture through the story of how their in-house language got its name.
